Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a normative process description and an event log with 5 cases We need to identify which cases violate the normative process and explain the violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First lets restate the normative process clearly The process always starts with Receive order Then Check inventory must occur before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Two main paths 1 Inventory sufficient normal flow Receive Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Constraints Authorize payment after Check inventory and before Package items Package items after Authorize payment and before Ship order Ship order after Package items Close order final

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2 Inventory insufficient cancellation flow Receive Check inventory Cancel order Close order After Cancel order no fulfillment activities Package items Ship order Authorize payment should occur Close order is final

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Additional constraints from text Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order This follows the normal flow exactly No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Here Package items occurs before Authorize payment According to normative process Package items should only occur after authorization So this is a violation packaging before payment authorization

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order This case has Authorize payment then Cancel order then Ship order According to normative process if inventory insufficient after Check inventory we should go directly to Cancel order

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: But here they did Authorize payment before Cancel order That might be a violation Lets read if the inventory check reveals that there is not enough stock to fulfill the order the process must branch into a cancellation scenario In such a case after checking the inventory the process should directly proceed to Cancel order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So after Check inventory if insufficient it should go directly to Cancel order Here they did Authorize payment before Cancel order That suggests they authorized payment even though inventory might be insufficient

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also after Cancel order they did Ship order The text says Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation So Ship order after Cancel order is a clear violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also they didnt do Package items before Ship order Actually they didnt have Package items at all Ship order after Cancel order and without packaging is a violation So Case 3 has multiple violations Authorize payment before Cancel order if inventory insufficient should cancel directly and Ship order after Cancel order never after cancellation and Ship order without packaging

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order This follows the cancellation flow exactly No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Here after Authorize payment they go directly to Ship order skipping Package items The normative process says Package items should occur after authorization and before shipping So missing Package items is a violation Also Ship order should only occur after packaging So this is a violation
